---
pagetitle: "jstats:Windows"
title-prefix: ""
---

## Windows setup

A little one-time preparation gets your Windows computer ready to install jstats
while it's distributed from GitHub. It takes a few minutes, and once jstats
reaches CRAN none of it will be needed.

{{< include _package-basics.qmd >}}

## Install Rtools (recommended)

**Rtools** is a small set of build tools for Windows. jstats itself doesn't need
it — jstats is written entirely in R, so it installs and runs without compiling
anything. But there's a practical reason to install it now anyway. While jstats
installs from GitHub, RStudio shows a warning every time you install *or update* a
package if it can't find Rtools on your computer. The warning begins *"Rtools is
required to build R packages but is not currently installed"* and looks alarming —
but it is harmless here: nothing is actually wrong, and the install still works.
The catch is that it reappears on every update, and jstats updates often during
this phase, so the simplest way never to be bothered by it is to install Rtools
once, now.

To install it:

1. Go to <https://cran.r-project.org/bin/windows/Rtools/>.
2. Choose the version that **matches the version of R you installed** — for
   example **Rtools45** for R 4.5.x, or **Rtools44** for R 4.4.x. (The Rtools
   page explains how to tell which you need. Matching matters — an unmatched
   version won't clear the warning.)
3. Run the installer with its **default settings**, then **restart RStudio**
   (close it and reopen it) so it picks up the new tools.

After this, the Rtools warning won't appear when you install or update jstats.

::: {.callout-note collapse="true" title="What the warning looks like (if you skip Rtools)"}
If you'd rather not install Rtools, you don't strictly have to — jstats works
perfectly without it. You'll just see this warning each time you install or
update jstats, beginning *"Rtools is required to build R packages but is not
currently installed"*:

![The Rtools warning RStudio shows when Rtools isn't installed. It is harmless — the install still completes.](images/rtools-warning-install.png)

It's safe to ignore: jstats is pure R, so nothing is being compiled, and the
install finishes normally. The same warning reappears on every update:

![The same Rtools warning, shown during an update. Harmless — the update still completes.](images/rtools-warning-update.png)

Installing Rtools (above) is simply the way to stop it appearing.
:::

## Next: install jstats

With Rtools in place (or knowingly skipped), continue to
[Step 5 — Install jstats](install-jstats.qmd), where the actual install commands
live. Once jstats moves to CRAN, this preparation page won't be needed —
installation will happen the ordinary way, with no warning and no Rtools.
